name: #57: Activating Ambassadors
description:
As I started diving head-first today into Always’ broader business intelligence (analytics, reporting), I’ve realized there’s a foundational piece of architecture that needs to be established first: formally integrating Ambassadors into the system.

Up until now, the correct focus has been on building out our Affiliate infrastructure. That’s now in a great place. Affiliates can choose whether or not they receive commissions; we can set custom percentages on the admin side... now we’re ready for the other tier in our growth model: Ambassadors.

At Always, Ambassadors are distinct from Affiliates. They are active in the field, cold-calling, running and hosting events, introducing new Members, and recruiting Affiliates. Because of this, their compensation structure is layered:
  • 30% commission on in-person sales at events**
  • 5% residual override on new Members that come in through their Affiliates.
Just like with Affiliates, this % can be modified by Admin.
I believe we already have the Ambassador user role created, but no accounts have been set up yet. Our next step is to:
  • Activate this role in practice (creating test Ambassador accounts for internal use)
  • Define how Ambassadors are attached to Members and Affiliates.
(And we do have two Ambassadors to activate once we're set up... currently, they're set up as Affiliates.)

Once this structure is in place, it will unlock the reporting and analytics we ultimately want (revenue by event, by Ambassador, by Affiliate, and by channel), which is core to how Always will scale this year.

**Although the website only tracks online sales, I'm curious (once we have a BI Dashboard set up) if we can have a way to enter sales after each event manually.  I do not believe Clover has an API that sends sales data out, but this data would be easy to enter, and a very important business metric.

Anyway, I’ll follow this email with my thoughts on Always Business Intelligence (Analytics and Reporting), but I wanted to start by clearly framing why this step comes first.

description:

We need to set up how Event Waiver signatories are handled in Supabase and synchronized with Klaviyo.

At every event, all attendees must sign a waiver (for free samples) regardless of whether they are already a Member or Affiliate.

The waiver captures their name, birthdate, and email address and Opt-In status.  We now need to operationalize this data for marketing (to those who Opted-In) while maintaining clean separation from existing users.

I'm looking for:
  1. Waiver Signatories List synced with Klayvio (separate list segment) for those who Opted-In.
  2. Waiver Signatories List updated (name removed) whenever one becomes a member, and updated (name added) when new non-Member's sign waivers at monthly events.
  3. All website users (Members, Affiliates, etc) who sign waiver are NOT added to Waiver Signatories List.  Users emails must be permanently removed (or suppressed) from the Waiver Signatories marketing list.
Once this is established, we will be able to build a drip campaign targeting this list with light-touch monthly emails, reminding them they have memership access, CTA to apply.

Let me know if there are any concerns. Thank you!

name: #66: Subscription + Purchase Order Flow Issues
description:
Hi team,

Here is a raised concern from the client:

I want to flag an issue that surfaced last night with Order #321 (see screenshot below). This revealed some structural gaps in our subscription and payment flow that need attention.



Here’s what happened:

A member placed:
  • A subscription for the new gummy flavor
  • A couple of one-time sample pack purchases

She accidentally selected Purchase Order instead of Credit Card, so no payment was collected at checkout.

When I followed up, she confirmed it was an honest mistake.

The complication is that because this order included a subscription and no credit card was captured, there is currently no payment method on file for future rebills.

My understanding is:
  • Unless we cancel and have her reorder correctly,
  • The subscription will continue generating as a “manual payment” each billing cycle,
  • And I would need to manually collect payment each month.

Please confirm whether that is accurate.  I'm moving forward with cancellation today.

Subscription Visibility Issues


While reviewing this, I discovered larger concerns:
1. In the Subscriptions section, I cannot clearly see:
• What product each subscription is for
• A clean summary of active subscriptions by product

2. When clicking into a subscription, it redirects to the original order.
• Many of these orders contain multiple items.
• Only one of those items is actually on subscription.
• I have to manually reverse-engineer the pricing to determine which item is recurring.

This is inefficient and prone to error.

This is friction I don't want.  It took me approximately 20 minutes to determine why shipping was not charged (order was under $200, but subscription logic altered the flow), and then more time to look into the subscriptions themselve to understand whats up.  I didn't even know we had four new subscriptions this month.

What This Highlights
We need:
  • Clear indication in the Order view that a subscription is attached
  • Perhaps, before we move into adding Business Intelligence dashboards or anything... can we add a basic view on the current dashboard showing Subscription details:
    • Product
    • Price
    • Billing frequency
    • Payment method on file
    • Status
    • Recurring Monthly Rev from Subs
  • Automatic notification when a new subscription is created is not necessary if we can see it on the Order View.

Right now, the subscription system feels overly basic and not built for even minimal operational management.  I don't need super high tech, I just need some fundamental insights.

Please review and make suggestions for the next steps.

name: #75: Team Roles and Permissions
description:
I'm curious whether Medusa’s current default role handling is purely cosmetic in our implementation (which I believe Jun told me it was), or whether any real permission logic is already in place.

Because I’d like to move forward with a new development ticket for our admin system.

We need to implement a proper internal roles and permissions structure for the Always admin/backend environment.  Ticket #76 sparked this for me, because I want some genuine Member access control, but ONLY ME.  I'd like some lesser options for access to be available for Josh, Trenton and Danniel, but right now the current role setup doesn't provide for any differentiation here.

Once a team user is assigned a role, I don't have the ability to change that person’s role from the admin side. I can only edit their first name, last name, and email address. Any role change on Always Medusa users or any member access issues (i.e., Ticket 74) currently requires involvement from our dev team, which is not sustainable (especially with the time change).  I want Always to be able to provide very timely and top tier customer support.

We would like to formalize a simple internal staff roles to the following four roles only:
• Super Admin
• Ops Admin
• Support
• Developer

The goal is to create a real permissions system behind these roles, not just labels.

Here is the intended structure at a high level:

Super Admin (Only Me)
This role should have full access and full authority across the system. This includes the ability to assign and change staff roles, manage all member access tools, and access all sensitive administrative controls. This role will only be for me for now and will be reserved for future ownership-level control.

Ops Admin (Josh)
This role should have strong operational access for day-to-day management, but should not have full system authority. This may include customer and membership management, order-related actions, and other operational tools, while excluding the highest-risk permissions such as assigning Super Admin or accessing the most sensitive security controls.

Support (Danniel & Trenton)
This role should focus solely on customer/member assistance and product creation, discounts, channels, etc... It should have limited access to tools needed to help users regain access or resolve account issues, but should not be able to modify staff roles, access advanced configuration, or use high-risk administrative functions.

Developer (You guys!) - All the things!

What we need built:
1. A real internal permissions structure tied to these roles.
2. The ability for a Super Admin to assign roles when inviting a user.
3. The ability for a Super Admin to change an existing team user’s role later from within the admin interface.
4. Clear role-based restrictions on which features and actions are visible or usable by each role.
5. A clean and sustainable structure that can support future administrative tools, including member access recovery tools.

My main concern here is that we need proper operational control on our side without having to submit development tickets every time a team user’s permissions need to be adjusted.

Please let me know your recommendation for the best way to implement it within our current Medusa/Supabase setup.

Thank you!

name: Newsletter Module – Multiple Issues Found During Validation (Prod)
description:
During validation of the Newsletter module, multiple issues were identified across campaign editing, subscriber management, imports, and analytics. These issues were found while the feature is still not live in production.

Issues Identified

  1. Campaign Editor

    • Campaign content is removed when the template is changed inside the editor.

  2. Subscriber Management

    • Removing subscribers does not work correctly.

    • No success confirmation is shown after removal.

    • Subscriber list shows inconsistent results after removal.

  3. Subscriber Import

    • Import subscribers is not working.

    • Get import status is not working (blocked due to import failure).

  4. Analytics

    • Campaign analytics and performance tracking are stuck on loading.

  5. Newsletter Templates

    • Newsletter templates section is stuck on loading.

Expected Behavior

  • Campaign content should persist when switching templates.

  • Subscribers should be removed successfully with proper confirmation and consistent list updates.

  • Subscriber import should complete successfully and return a valid import status.

  • Campaign analytics and newsletter templates should load correctly.

Actual Behavior

  • Campaign content is cleared on template change.

  • Subscriber removal behaves inconsistently.

  • Import functionality does not work, blocking import status checks.

  • Analytics and newsletter templates remain stuck on loading.

Notes

  • Issues were found during initial validation.

  • Feature is not yet live in production.

  • Logged early to track and address defects before release.

name: Previous Version Incorrectly Tagged as Published After New Version Published
description:
The previous version (v.6) of the content is currently tagged as Published, even though a newer version has already been published.

This creates confusion regarding which version is the active live version.

Issue Details:

  • The older version still shows the Published status.

  • A newer version of the same content has already been released and should be the only version marked as Published.

  • This may cause incorrect version tracking and content status visibility.

Expected Behavior:

  • Only the latest version should be marked as Published.

  • Previous versions should automatically be set to Archived, Superseded, or another appropriate non-published status.

Actual Behavior:

  • Both the previous and newer versions appear as published.

name: GSC Data
description:
Hope you are doing well! We have been digging into our Google Search Console data across all six of our practice websites and found one issue that keeps showing up that I wanted to flag.


For most of our sites, Google is seeing two separate versions of the homepage and treating them as different pages. For example on Grand Rapids, it is seeing both:
  • http://www.grandrapidsdentalteam.com/
  • https://grandrapidsdentalteam.com/


Because Google thinks these are two different pages, the ranking strength is being split between them instead of all going to one URL. Consolidating these would help our search rankings.


The same thing is happening on these four sites:
  • Grand Rapids Dental Team
  • Grandville Dental Team
  • Livonia Dental Team
  • Toledo Dental Team


It would see the fix is making sure all traffic redirects consistently to a single version of the homepage URL. Our preference would be https, non-www for all sites (e.g. https://grandrapidsdentalteam.com/) but happy to defer to whatever you recommend as the cleanest solution.

Waterford and Willoughby look clean on this front so no changes needed there.

One other small item…on the Waterford site, a Sanity CDN image URL is showing up as an indexed page in Google Search Console. The URL looks something like:

https://www.waterforddentalteam.com/_next/image?url=https://cdn.sanity.io/images/...


Not a big deal but wanted to mention it in case there is an easy way to prevent those from being indexed.

That is it! Really appreciate everything you do for us. Let me know if you have any questions.

Hi Jerry, 

Thanks for your response and patience as we look further into resolving your concern.

I've reviewed your Stripe Tax setup and can help you resolve this issue. To properly collect all applicable taxes:

1. Enable Automatic Tax: When creating Payment Intents via API, make sure to include automatic_tax[enabled]=true in your API calls. This parameter is essential for Stripe to calculate all applicable taxes.
2. Collect Complete Customer Addresses: Stripe Tax needs the customer's complete address (including city/county information) to calculate the correct local taxes. Make sure you're collecting and passing this information with each transaction.
3. California Tax Collection: For web sales in California, you don't need to register separately for each city/county. Your existing California registration allows Stripe Tax to automatically calculate and collect the appropriate local taxes based on your customer's address.

Please refer to the links below:

https://docs.stripe.com/tax/payment-intent
https://docs.stripe.com/tax/custom#calculate-tax

I hope the above information helps. I'll keep the ticket open to ensure the issue is properly resolved, and the tax is calculated accordingly once the registration has been added.

Hi Jerry,

Thank you for speaking with me earlier about the local tax collection issue. It seems like our line got disconnected. I tried calling you again, but I wasn’t able to connect with you.

I've reviewed your account and can explain why local city/county taxes weren't collected on your first transaction. I'd like to provide a detailed explanation of how to ensure your Stripe account properly collects city and county taxes.

Upon checking, when you first set up Stripe Tax, your account was likely configured to collect state-level California taxes, but not the specific local taxes for Atherton/San Mateo County. For California transactions, Stripe Tax needs explicit registration for each local tax district to collect those additional taxes.

To ensure you collect all applicable local taxes on future transactions:
1. Go to Dashboard > Tax > Registrations
2. Click "Add a registration"
3. Select California
4. During setup, make sure to add any specific city/county jurisdictions where you're registered.

For your first transaction, you'll need to manually account for any uncollected local taxes when filing your tax return.

Please know that I'll keep the ticket open to ensure the issue is properly resolved, and the tax is calculated accordingly once the registration has been added.
